text.title: Design System AI Workflows
text.summary: Component documentation pipeline, token audit workflow, and variant generation rules with quality gates.

list.item: 1. Haiku subagent reads component source (Storybook stories, TypeScript props, Figma export JSON)
2. Generates documentation: component purpose, props table, usage examples, accessibility notes, do/don't
3. Component engineer reviews for accuracy — verifies props, interaction states, ARIA behavior
4. Design system lead approves before merging to published system

rule.statement: Documentation generation works best on components with TypeScript prop types — poorly typed components produce poor documentation regardless of AI quality.
rule.rationale: AI documentation quality is directly coupled to the quality of the code it reads. TypeScript types are the contract the agent uses to produce the props table.

list.item: 1. Audit script scans codebase for hardcoded hex colors and px values
2. Sonnet subagent produces replacement report with suggested token names and confidence ratings
3. Low-confidence items flagged for human review
4. Design system lead approves token replacement list before developers apply changes

rule.statement: Feed the agent the token JSON and naming convention document as context before requesting any token suggestions — without it, AI invents names that violate the system's conventions.
rule.rationale: Without the token registry as context, the agent cannot know whether a token already exists or what the naming pattern is. It will generate new token names that duplicate existing ones or violate conventions.

rule.statement: Run Chromatic visual regression testing on every AI-generated variant before merging — AI-generated code often has subtle visual differences from the design spec.
rule.rationale: AI-generated variants look correct in code review but may have pixel-level rendering differences. Chromatic catches these before they reach production.

rule.statement: Require agents to generate disabled, focus, and error states independently — a prompt saying "generate variants" produces only visual variants, missing interaction states.
rule.rationale: Interaction states are not variants in the visual sense; they require different ARIA attributes, keyboard handling, and visual treatment. Agents omit them unless explicitly required.

text.reference: Bash script to find hardcoded hex colors and px spacing
Haiku prompt for component documentation generation
Sonnet prompt for token inconsistency detection
